About

A firm believer that the internet industry has a long way to go, so developing more and more innovative and creative applications and solutions that fulfill the requirements of today's society.   • Anshul Goel

    10mo •

    Fascinating and important concept for training Machines And AI about time developed by my nephew Krish Goel and his friends.

    It's inspiring to see young innovators leveraging technology for progress and steering it in a positive direction. I warmly welcome any advice or feedback on their research!

    Krish Goel

    • 3rd+

    Learning to think, and teaching it to machines, sometimes robots. Memory Research @ Littlebird AI.

    10mo • Edited •

    5 CS undergrads, no lab, no supervision, no faculty and an year of "figuring it out" later, we're super excited to present to you - "Chronocept".

    It's the world's first benchmark to instill a sense of time in machines.

    Chronocept tackles a core limitation in current AI systems: they don’t know how long information remains valid.

    Humans intuitively know that “a doctor's appointment lasts anywhere from 15 minutes to an hour", but “a drive through heavy traffic would likely take a couple hours (and not 15 seconds or an year)". Chronocept helps machines learn that too.

    We reformulate temporal validity prediction as a continuous regression task.
    Each input (a sentence or paragraph) is -
    - Segmented into 8 semantic temporal axes (Main Outcome, Static, Generic, Hypothetical, Negation, Intention, Opinion, and Recurrent)
    - Fitted to a skewed-normal distribution over logarithmic time, with three parameters:
    • Location (ξ): when validity peaks
    • Scale (ω): how long it lasts
    • Skewness (α): how it rises or fades
    This lets models capture nuanced phenomena like gradual decay, delayed onset, and asymmetric relevance - beyond what binary or timestamp-based models can do.

    We release two carefully constructed benchmarks -
    - Benchmark I: 1,254 atomic factual statements
    - Benchmark II: 524 multi-sentence paragraphs with intertwined temporal logic

    Annotations were done manually by 8 rigorously trained annotators with multiple review rounds, under strict protocols. We report high inter-annotator agreements: 84% and 89% across the two benchmarks, despite a nuanced three-step annotation protocol.

    Chronocept is built to power -
    - RAG systems
    - Fact checkers
    - Knowledge bases
    - Proactive assistants and agents (knowing when to speak, not just what)
    It's designed to plug into ML systems at the application layer, and help make time-aware decisions.

    Here's everything -
    - Publication:     https://lnkd.in/dKSi3Nh6
    - Datasets: https://lnkd.in/dvdGsg8M
    - Trained Baselines and a DataLoader: https://lnkd.in/d8PiMGRm

    This ones for the team - Sanskar, Mahadevan K S, Harsh Kumar, Vishesh Khadaria, and the annotators. You guys rock!

    This is our lives' best work, and we hope to say this about everything we do hereinafter.

    Chronocept: Instilling a Sense of Time in MachinesChronocept: Instilling a Sense of Time in Machines

    arxiv.org

        Ankur Warikoo

        • 2nd

        Founder @WebVeda, @IndiaGeniusChallenge • Speaker • 6X Bestselling Author • 16M+ community

        Visit my website

        4yr • Edited •

        Gifting teenagers needs to change. 

        More often than not, we gift them gadgets, clothes, shoes - making them believe that status is everything.

        How about gifting them books, stocks, and courses?

        For his birthday, I recently gifted my nephew a         FamPay me card - which is a card for teenagers. Growing up in a family where money is rarely talked about, I am excited how this could set him up on a path of financial independence at the age of 15 itself!

        The other day, someone mentioned they have turned 18 and I asked them to open up a Zerodha brokerage account and I will gift them a Reliance stock. That stock is not going to change his life, but it can surely set them up on a path of investing. 

        Gifts for teenagers need a rethink!

        If you are the gifter, consider what you are gifting them and what lesson are you sharing with them indirectly?

        #gift #teenagers #stocks #warikoo
